title: Lesson Quiz: What Is Digital Citizenship?
description: Assessment of key concepts aligned to the lesson objective.

choices.text: Using technology only for school assignments
choices.feedback: Digital citizenship applies to all online behavior, not just school use.

choices.text: Participating safely, responsibly, and respectfully online
choices.feedback: Correct. This is the core definition of digital citizenship.
Digital citizenship focuses on responsible participation, not avoidance.

choices.text: Following rules only when being monitored
choices.feedback: Responsible behavior should be consistent, not dependent on monitoring.

questions.prompt: Digital citizenship includes how people behave both online and offline when using technology.

choices.feedback: Correct. Digital citizenship includes behavior connected to technology use in all contexts.
Digital citizenship includes all behavior related to technology use.

questions.prompt: Which is an example of responsible digital citizenship?

choices.feedback: Responsible users consider consequences before posting.

choices.text: Respecting others and following guidelines online
choices.feedback: Correct. Respect and responsibility are key components.
Not all information should be shared.
Following rules is part of responsible behavior.

choices.text: It helps people avoid all digital tools
choices.feedback: The goal is responsible use, not avoidance.

choices.text: It ensures safe, respectful, and effective use of technology
choices.feedback: Correct. Digital citizenship promotes safe and responsible technology use.
Time spent online is not the focus.
Risks can be managed, not eliminated.

questions.prompt: Which action reflects good digital citizenship?

choices.text: Thinking about the impact of posts before sharing
choices.feedback: Correct. Responsible users consider impact before posting.
